content:
AA From FIR to Final Judgment: Understanding the Complete BNSS Trial Process
As law students, we often memorize section numbers but miss the bigger picture-how a criminal case actually moves through the justice system.
To bridge that gap, I created a Chronological & Procedural BNSS Trial Process Chart that simplifies the journey of a criminal case under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 (BNSS).
The chart covers:
* Preventive Proceedings
* FIR Registration
* Investigation
• Arrest & Rights of the Accused
* Remand & Bail
* Charge Sheet
* Cognizance by the Court
• Framing of Charge
• Trial & Recording of Evidence
* Statement of the Accused
* Defence Evidence
* Final Arguments
& Judgment & Sentencing
• Appeals & Execution of Sentence
Understanding the chronology of criminal procedure is just as important as knowing individual sections. A strong legal professional doesn't merely remember statutory provisions-they understand why each stage exists, who exercises the power, what procedural safeguards apply, and how every step connects to the next.

comment: In practice the BNSS timeline isn’t linear—remand extensions, supplementary charge sheets and cognizance delays often blur stages and directly shape bail strategy and trial pace
